timePeriod.description: The time period covered by this summary, with inclusive start time and exclusive end time.

startTimeInclusive.description: The start time of the period, inclusive. Events at or after this time are included.

endTimeExclusive.description: The end time of the period, exclusive. Events before this time are included.

total.description: The aggregated totals for this summary, including event count and estimated savings.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination. If present, indicates there are more results available and can be used in subsequent requests.

ruleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the automation rule.

ruleId.description: The unique identifier of the automation rule.

ruleType.description: The type of automation rule (OrganizationRule or AccountRule).

ruleRevision.description: The revision number of the automation rule.

accountId.description: The 12-digit Amazon Web Services account ID that owns this automation rule.

ruleApplyOrder.description: Specifies when organization rules should be applied relative to account rules.

accountIds.description: List of specific Amazon Web Services account IDs where the organization rule should be applied.

priority.description: A string representation of a decimal number between 0 and 1 (having up to 30 digits after the decimal point) that determines the priority of the rule. When multiple rules match the same recommended action, Compute Optimizer assigns the action to the rule with the lowest priority value (highest priority), even if that rule is scheduled to run later than other matching rules.

recommendedActionTypes.description: List of recommended action types that this rule can execute.

schedule.description: The schedule configuration for when the automation rule should execute.

scheduleExpression.description:
The expression that defines when the schedule runs. cron expression is supported. A cron expression consists of six fields separated by white spaces: (minutes hours day_of_month month day_of_week year)

You can schedule rules to run at most once per day. Your cron expression must use specific values (not wildcards) for the minutes and hours fields. For example: (30 12 * * *) runs daily at 12:30 PM UTC.

scheduleExpressionTimezone.description: The timezone to use when interpreting the schedule expression.

executionWindowInMinutes.description: The time window in minutes during which the automation rule can start implementing recommended actions.

status.description: The current status of the automation rule (Active or Inactive).

createdTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the automation rule was created.

lastUpdatedTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the automation rule was last updated.

total.description: Aggregate totals for the recommended actions in this group, including count and estimated savings.

ebsVolume.description: Detailed configuration information specific to EBS volumes, including volume type, size, IOPS, and throughput settings.

configuration.description: The configuration details of the EBS volume, including type, size, IOPS, and throughput.

type.description: The EBS volume type, such as gp2, gp3, io1, io2, st1, or sc1.

sizeInGib.description: The size of the EBS volume in gibibytes (GiB).

iops.description: The number of I/O operations per second (IOPS) provisioned for the volume.

throughput.description: The throughput in MiB/s provisioned for the volume (applicable to gp3, io1, and io2bx volumes).

name.description: The name of the automation rule. Must be 1-128 characters long and contain only alphanumeric characters, underscores, and hyphens.

description.description: A description of the automation rule. Can be up to 1024 characters long and contain alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, spaces, and certain special characters.

ruleType.description: The type of automation rule. Can be either OrganizationRule for organization-wide rules or AccountRule for account-specific rules.

ruleRevision.description: The revision number of the automation rule. This is incremented each time the rule is updated.

organizationConfiguration.description: Configuration settings for organization-wide rules, including rule application order and target account IDs.

priority.description: The priority level of the automation rule, used to determine execution order when multiple rules apply to the same resource.

criteria.description: A set of conditions that specify which recommended action qualify for implementation. When a rule is active and a recommended action matches these criteria, Compute Optimizer implements the action at the scheduled run time. You can specify up to 20 conditions per filter criteria and 20 values per condition.

schedule.description: The schedule configuration for when the automation rule should execute, including cron expression, timezone, and execution window.

tags.description: A list of key-value pairs used to categorize and organize the automation rule. Maximum of 200 tags allowed.
